title: gonocarpus oreophilus
intro: Gonocarpus oreophilus, also known as the rock dodder, is a species of flowering plant in the family Haloragaceae, native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Gonocarpus oreophilus include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
